_comment: Examples under datrix/examples/ that the REAL generation pipeline cannot build today.
This is a DELIBERATELY MAINTAINED SCOPE BOUNDARY, not an auto-heal: it converts a genuine,
pre-existing generation FAILURE into a loudly-reported skip in the reference-example parity
gate (datrix/scripts/test/reference-example-parity-gate.ps1). It never hides output drift --
a listed example that does generate is still hash-compared against its baseline.
Both entries reproduce with the sanctioned generator (generate.ps1 <system.dtrx> <out> -L python),
so neither is a gate artifact. Both are also ABSENT from scripts/config/test-projects.json, which
is why generate.ps1 -All never built them and the defects went unnoticed.
expected_count is a pinned, reviewed value: change it only in the same change that adds or
removes an entry. Removing an entry means the defect is FIXED and the example must be blessed.

examples.02-features-02-service-architecture-pubs: ConfigResolutionError ING001: the system declares a gateway {} block but no service derives ServiceIngressExposure.GATEWAY (a front door with nothing behind it). Example-definition defect: the example declares no external-caller auth(...) endpoint. Unregistered in test-projects.json, so generate.ps1 -All never built it. Follow-up: FIX-EXAMPLE-REPLAYABLE-INGESTION-ING001.
examples.02-features-03-infrastructure-blocks-con: ValueError from build_runtime_bootstrap: RuntimeBootstrap LOCAL requires config_store engine 'file' (platform 'container'), but the example declares engine='consul' platform='container'. The example's declared config store is incompatible with the LOCAL runtime-bootstrap contract. Unregistered in test-projects.json, so generate.ps1 -All never built it. Follow-up: FIX-EXAMPLE-CONFIG-STORE-CONSUL-BOOTSTRAP.
